svg.style: background: #ffffff; background-color: light-dark(#ffffff, var(--ge-dark-color, #121212)); color-scheme: light dark;
svg.content: <mxfile host="app.diagrams.net" agent="5.0 (X11; Linux x86_64) AppleWebKit/537.36 (KHTML, like Gecko) Chrome/95.0.4638.69 Safari/537.36" scale="1" border="0"> <diagram id="Pg1SmI05z0wTsQ_Bj4LX" name="Page-1"> <mxGraphModel dx="3923" dy="2837" grid="1" gridSize="10" guides="1" tooltips="1" connect="1" arrows="1" fold="1" page="1" pageScale="1" pageWidth="827" pageHeight="1169" math="0" shadow="0"> <root> <mxCell id="0" /> <mxCell id="1" parent="0" /> <mxCell id="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-1" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" value="<b><font style="font-size: 14px">Service Worker</font></b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="60" width="120" x="230" y="100" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-5" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fontSize=13;align=left;fillColor=#fff2cc;strokeColor=#d6b656;" value="<span style="color: rgb(33 , 33 , 33) ; font-family: &#34;arial&#34; ; background-color: rgb(255 , 255 , 255)"><font style="font-size: 14px">A service worker is a small JavaScript-based program or rather to say a worker that can run in the background and can intercepts any request, like HTTP requests. It is based on the worker implementation where we can decide what need to do with these requests. Also, using a Service worker, we can implement the caching for the application. So, the service worker function act as a network proxy. It intercepts all outgoing HTTP requests made through the application and then decides how to respond to them. Like, for example, it can query to the local cache and deliver the cached response against any HTTP request if that exists. So, the Service worker-based caching is programmable, and it doesn’t depend on the server-oriented caching layers.</font></span>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="160" width="580" x="30" y="-100" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-6" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;fontSize=14;" value="<b>Step 1</b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="120" </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-49" edge="1" parent="1" source="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-40" style="endArrow=classic;html=1;rounded=0;sketch=1;fontSize=14;exitX=0.642;exitY=1.05;exitDx=0;exitDy=0;exitPerimeter=0;entryX=0.056;entryY=0.013;entryDx=0;entryDy=0;entryPerimeter=0;" target="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-45" value=""> <mxGeometry height="50" relative="1" width="50" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="1520" y="260" as="sourcePoint" /> <mxPoint x="1570" y="210" as="targetPoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <mxCell id="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-52" parent="1" style="text;html=1;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=left;verticalAlign=middle;whiteSpace=wrap;rounded=0;sketch=1;fontSize=14;" value="<div>Now the If you want to block the network connection so that the chrome browser does not have any network access.</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div>For applications that do not use the Angular service worker, refreshing now would display Chrome's Internet disconnected&nbsp;</div><div>page that says "There is no Internet connection". With the addition of an Angular service worker, the application behavior changes.</div><div>On a refresh, the page loads normally.</div><div>&nbsp;</div><div>If you look at the Network tab, you can verify that the service worker is active.</div><div><br></div><div>Notice that under the "Size" column, the requests state is (from Service-Worker). This means that the resources are not being&nbsp;</div><div>loaded from the network. Instead, they are being loaded from the service worker's cache.</div><div><br></div>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="270" width="560" x="1440" y="1060" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-53" parent="1" style="rounded=0;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;sketch=1;fontSize=14;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;align=left;" value="<ol style="box-sizing: border-box ; color: rgb(33 , 33 , 33) ; font-family: &#34;arial&#34; ; background-color: rgb(255 , 255 , 255)"><li style="box-sizing: border-box">We can perform caching an application is just like installing a native application. The entire application is cached as one unit, and all files update together.</li><li style="box-sizing: border-box">The running application can be run with the same version of all the files. It does not suddenly start receiving the cached files from a newer version, which may occur to unstable the application.</li><li style="box-sizing: border-box">When the user refreshes the application, they normally see the latest fully cached version of the application. New tabs of the browser also load the latest cached code.</li><li style="box-sizing: border-box">In the cached application, the new updates always happened in the background when the changes are published. The previous version of the application always served until an update is installed and ready.</li><li style="box-sizing: border-box">The service worker can conserve the bandwidth when possible. Resources are only be downloaded if they have changed in the source.</li></ol>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="190" width="670" x="490" y="970" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-54" parent="1" style="rounded=1;whiteSpace=wrap;html=1;sketch=1;fontSize=14;fillColor=#dae8fc;strokeColor=#6c8ebf;" value="<b><font style="font-size: 17px">Benefits</font></b>" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="100" x="760" y="900" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> <mxCell id="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-55" edge="1" parent="1" source="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-54" style="shape=flexArrow;endArrow=classic;html=1;rounded=0;sketch=1;fontSize=17;exitX=0.41;exitY=1.075;exitDx=0;exitDy=0;exitPerimeter=0;entryX=0.46;entryY=0.009;entryDx=0;entryDy=0;entryPerimeter=0;fillColor=#d5e8d4;strokeColor=#82b366;" target="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-53" value=""> <mxGeometry height="50" relative="1" width="50" as="geometry"> <mxPoint x="940" y="960" as="sourcePoint" /> <mxPoint x="990" y="910" as="targetPoint" /> </mxGeometry> </mxCell> <UserObject label="Important link" link="https://www.c-sharpcorner.com/article/service-worker-why-required-and-how-to-implement-it-in-angular-project/" id="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-56"> <mxCell parent="1" style="text;html=1;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;whiteSpace=wrap;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;fontColor=#0000EE;fontStyle=4;sketch=1;fontSize=14;" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="40" width="120" x="-260" y="1040" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </UserObject> <mxCell id="76nHn2v8Cyzsj7EThvP0-57" parent="1" style="text;html=1;strokeColor=none;fillColor=none;align=center;verticalAlign=middle;whiteSpace=wrap;rounded=0;sketch=1;fontSize=14;" value="https://www.c-sharpcorner.com/article/service-worker-why-required-and-how-to-implement-it-in-angular-project/" vertex="1"> <mxGeometry height="30" width="770" x="-390" y="960" as="geometry" /> </mxCell> </root> </mxGraphModel> </diagram> </mxfile>

span.style: color: light-dark(rgb(33, 33, 33), rgb(209, 209, 209)); font-family: arial; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
span.font: A service worker is a small JavaScript-based program or rather to say a worker that can run in the background and can intercepts any request, like HTTP requests. It is based on the worker implementation where we can decide what need to do with these requests. Also, using a Service worker, we can implement the caching for the application. So, the service worker function act as a network proxy. It intercepts all outgoing HTTP requests made through the application and then decides how to respond to them. Like, for example, it can query to the local cache and deliver the cached response against any HTTP request if that exists. So, the Service worker-based caching is programmable, and it doesn’t depend on the server-oriented caching layers.

div.div: Now the If you want to block the network connection so that the chrome browser does not have any network access.
For applications that do not use the Angular service worker, refreshing now would display Chrome's Internet disconnected
page that says "There is no Internet connection". With the addition of an Angular service worker, the application behavior changes.
On a refresh, the page loads normally.
If you look at the Network tab, you can verify that the service worker is active.
Notice that under the "Size" column, the requests state is (from Service-Worker). This means that the resources are not being
loaded from the network. Instead, they are being loaded from the service worker's cache.

div.style: display: flex; align-items: unsafe center; justify-content: unsafe flex-start; width: 668px; height: 1px; padding-top: 1165px; margin-left: 882px;
box-sizing: border-box; font-size: 0; text-align: left; color: #000000;
display: inline-block; font-size: 14px; font-family: Helvetica; color: light-dark(#000000, #ffffff); line-height: 1.2; pointer-events: all; white-space: normal; word-wrap: normal;

ol.style: box-sizing: border-box; color: light-dark(rgb(33, 33, 33), rgb(209, 209, 209)); font-family: arial; background-color: light-dark(rgb(255, 255, 255), rgb(18, 18, 18));
ol.li: We can perform caching an application is just like installing a native application. The entire application is cached as one unit, and all files update together.
The running application can be run with the same version of all the files. It does not suddenly start receiving the cached files from a newer version, which may occur to unstable the application.
When the user refreshes the application, they normally see the latest fully cached version of the application. New tabs of the browser also load the latest cached code.

In the cached application, the new updates always happened in the background when the changes are published. The previous version of the application always served until an update is installed and ready.
The service worker can conserve the bandwidth when possible. Resources are only be downloaded if they have changed in the source.
